rulings.Case Title: LUIS ZARAGOZA ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED, with the following admonishment. No opposition filed. Despite requesting the Court to add decedent's legal heirs as plaintiffs to the case, no one is ever identified by name in either the Notice, MPA, or counsel's declaration. The only way to identify who Plaintiff seeks to add is by referencing the proposed FAC and the proposed order. The second deficiency is more significant. Based on the FAC, and only the FAC, Plaintiff appears to want to add Nicholas Zaragoza-also a legal heir-as a nominal defendant. While this may be permissible under CCP Section 382, his addition as a defendant does not appear anywhere in the moving papers; nor does any mention of CCP Section 382. Counsel is advised that the Court will not extend this courtesy in the future for an otherwise deficient motion, opposed or unopposed. IT IS ORDERED that DALE D. SPURRIER (dba California Boiler), previously sued as Does 5 and 302; and WESTERN MACARTHUR COMPANY, previously sued as Does 6 and 303, are to be added as defendants to this action. NICHOLAS ZARAGOZA is added as a nominal defendant pursuant to CCP Section 382. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint on the newly added defendants forthwith. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. It is further ordered that legal heirs NATALIE ZARAGOZA and ALEXANDRA ZARAGOZA shall be added as plaintiffs to this action. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on March 12, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required. If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: MAHMUT YUKSEL ET AL VS. AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL INDUSTRIES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiffs' Motion for Preference Pursuant to C.C.P. Section 36(d) is CONTINUED to March 26, 2024 for further briefing. Not later than March 15, 2024, Plaintiff to provide medical documentation from Plaintiff's Janaury 2024 visit, along with a supplemental declaration from Dr. Adler discussing Plaintiff's current health status in light of the recent CT scans. Defendants may file a response, not more than 2 pages, by March 20, 2024. Additionally, Plaintiff must comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide complete courtesy copies of Plaintiff's "Index of Exhibits" no later than March 12, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Exhibit A to the Adler declaration is incomplete and Exhibits B-D were not included. All exhibits should be separately tabbed. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on March 12, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely. Face coverings are optional. Remote appearances must be arranged through CourtCall (1-888-88-COURT). No prior notice or permission is required.If a party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter, who must be licensed under Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 8016. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. =(301/RCE)
